panels.description: Card colour = health (up metric). Text = role (primary/replica).
panels.title: ${dc1_nodes}

panels.description: Card colour = health (up metric). Text = role.
panels.title: ${dc2_nodes}

panels.description: WAL bytes the DC2 upstream (pg4) is behind the DC1 primary. Remains populated even when the standby is fully disconnected from DC1 because it uses each node's local WAL position metric.
panels.title: Cross-DC Lag: Primary → DC2 Upstream (LSN bytes)

panels.description: WAL bytes each DC2 standby (pg5, pg6) is behind the DC2 upstream (pg4). Uses clamp_min to avoid negative values during brief window when pg4 lags behind standbys.
panels.title: DC2 Internal Lag: Upstream → Standbys (LSN bytes)
Replication Health (WAL Lag vs Primary)
panels.description: Per-replica WAL bytes behind the DC1 primary, using the local pg_wal_position metric. Stays populated even when a replica is fully disconnected — unlike pg_stat_replication which goes blank. Green <100 KB | Yellow <1 MB | Red ≥1 MB.

Lag computed as primary pg_current_wal_lsn minus each standby's pg_last_wal_replay_lsn. Reported by each node's own exporter independently — remains accurate even when the standby is fully disconnected from the primary.
panels.title: Replication Lag by LSN (bytes) — works when disconnected

panels.description: All postgres nodes that are actively reporting metrics. Green = reporting. A node disappearing from this panel means its postgres_exporter has stopped — distinct from replication lag.
panels.title: Node Presence
